headline: The dominant failure is stale or weak price data being allowed to drive totals recommendations. The Barcelona-style under picks were mostly price-quality failures, not a raw Over/Under mapping inversion.
dominantFlaw: Most of the ugliest recommendations were emitted on boards that were already too old or too thin to support a real bet. Current replay blocks those same cases as No Bet.
remainingRisks: Archived odds rows keep provider metadata but not raw source-side team labels, so source-team reconciliation for old price boards is still limited.
Per-snapshot rating state is not archived, so historical replay still uses the current active rating map for the rating component.
Historical finished-match odds coverage is still thin, so this audit is about recommendation logic, not proven historical ROI.

oddsBoards.note: Archived odds rows preserve provider metadata but not raw source-side team labels.

replay.downgradeReason: Stored odds are too old for the current kickoff window.

finalDecisionReason.originalSummary: Under 2.5 is the better angle. The game does not project as openly as the current number suggests.
finalDecisionReason.originalShortReason: The total is still a touch rich for a match that can spend long spells under control.
finalDecisionReason.originalRiskNote: An early goal would hurt the under fast.
finalDecisionReason.replayShortReason: No Bet. Stored odds are too old for the current kickoff window.
finalDecisionReason.replayRiskNote: The price board is too weak after a quota-degraded odds sync.
finalDecisionReason.replayPrimaryReason: No Bet across the three main markets.
